abstract: Initializes a graph with the specified list of nodes.
overview:
## Return Value

A new graph object.

## Discussion

The nodes in the array need not already be connected—you can connect nodes after adding them to the graph using the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/connectToLowestCostNode(node:bidirectional:)] method on the graph itself or the addConnection:bidirectional: method on individual nodes. Using the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method requires connections between nodes.

For more information, see [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

# init(_:)

**Initializer**

Initializes a graph with the specified list of nodes.

## Declaration

```swift
init(_ nodes: [GKGraphNode])
```

## Parameters

- **nodes**: An array of graph node objects—instances of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode] or of one of its subclasses containing geometry information.

## Return Value

A new graph object.

## Discussion

The nodes in the array need not already be connected—you can connect nodes after adding them to the graph using the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/connectToLowestCostNode(node:bidirectional:)] method on the graph itself or the addConnection:bidirectional: method on individual nodes. Using the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method requires connections between nodes.

For more information, see [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].
